candidates.stmt:
I've lived in Tamworth my whole life. I was raised and schooled in Stonydelph, and have called Belgrave home for the past 15 years.

Belgrave has been forgotten about. During my time living in the ward I've seen green spaces become unruly, community buildings become dilapidated and unusable, and important transport routes pretty much fall apart. I take pride in my home and I want to make sure we get our fair share of council funding to fix these issues.

My campaign policies are all about designing community-focused solutions that work for all residents within the ward and across the wider Tamworth borough.

I don't have the space to list them here, so head to facebook.com/greensforbelgrave to check them out!

I'm standing for the Green Party because I believe that the climate crisis, cost-of-living crisis, and societal unrest we're seeing are all part of the same problem. You can't solve one without tackling the other, and the Greens are the only party advocating for that!

To summarise, I'm a lifetime local representative with genuine compassion for the ward I'm standing in. I'll be a strong advocate for Belgrave, and I can't wait to help.

Please get in touch via greensforbelgrave@outlook.com or my social media channels to find out more 💚
Young, driven and ready to deliver for Bolehall. Fairer taxes - safer streets.

Lifelong Tamworth resident and builder, I’ve stood twice for election in Tamworth and I’m standing again because I believe I can make a difference by listening to you and using my real-world experience to deliver the kind of Tamworth that you want to see.
Local Government is changing and I want to be there to oversee a smooth transition, ensuring democracy at the local level is maintained, your voice is heard and our community heritage is preserved.
Labour and Conservative parties alike have consistently failed us, do you think the £21.6million “Levelling Up” funds were well spent in the town on some wonky new paving, some poky little shop units and a new college that was full to capacity on the day that it opened? Were you asked for your views on where this money should have been spent?
What we need is an environment that is well maintained, fostering pride in our community. Public transport that actually turns up when it is supposed to. Community banking hubs to replace the banks that are abandoning us. Public spaces where we don’t just survive, but thrive.
People want change, but don’t look in the direction of Reform to deliver it, since taking control of the County Council in May 2025 the only change they seem to have delivered is their leader (3 in the space of 4 months) every time a racist social media post comes to light. Theirs is the politics of Division, Isolation and Hate. Ours is of Inclusion, Community and Hope.
May 7th can be the turning point where we collectively send the message that we want something better not just for our future but for that of our children and generations to come.

I am Stephen Andrews, and I am standing as your local Green Party candidate for Spital Ward.

This by-election comes at a crucial time for Tamworth; we are about to see the largest change in how our council is run and how services are delivered in a generation. At a moment like this, Spital needs a strong, local voice that listens to residents, stands up for the community, and puts people first.

For too long, residents have been let down by the two-party system. The Conservatives and Labour have both failed to deliver for Tamworth. Reform are no different: they have no policies for Spital and no plan for Tamworth. I believe that we deserve better. We deserve honest representation that puts Tamworth first and prioritises our future.

I look at Spital and the failure of the past few years is evident:
•	You see the pain that leaseholders have on your everyday lives. 
•	You see the failure of the Government to hold water companies to account for pollution in our rivers and crumbling infrastructure, leading to floods that we’re all too familiar with.
•	You see the potential negative implications of new developments being built.

Instead, I want to be the voice to work towards change:
•	I will advocate for the end of the leasehold system; and work with residents who are struggling with getting essential repairs done.
•	I will put pressure on the water companies, making sure they are held accountable.
•	I will research, question, and challenge all new developments, ensuring the town’s best interests are at heart.

I am ready to put in the work to ensure that every voice is heard; together, we can make this happen.

You are choosing a new local councillor in the l upcoming local council by-election on Thursday 5th March.

We are being let down by our existing local councillors, they are saying one thing and then doing the opposite.  Whether it's Labour councillors promising to not cut free public events, and 
then cancelling the fireworks. Or Reform Councillors saying they'll cut your Council Tax and then raising it. You need a better Councillor.

I'm a hardworking local man who like many have been struggling. I work full time for the NHS and I deeply care about people. I'm a former councillor who knows how to get things done and have a strong  track record of positive change for my area, including £100,000's of investment into the roads and hundreds of fixed potholes, new and improved play areas and local community groups receing funding in the tens of thousands. That is what I bring as your candidate and what I want to do for Spital.
The upcoming local council by-election is a chance to vote for change.
A  vote for me is a vote to invigorate our town centre and support small businesses. A vote for me is a vote to support local households who need help from the council, not council tax rises. A vote for me is a way to get a community champion elected to the council who is focused on tackling  issues like potholes, over development and traffic.

These are the issues I will focus on. What I offer is experience at scrutinising council decisions and uncovering hidden issues that will affect us all. I want 
to stop things like wasting public money and hiding decisions away from the public.

I will shine a light on the decisions being made that are hidden from you,  like Reforms County Councillors delaying flood prevention strategies and 
local road repairs and Labour Borough Councillors cutting more free town centre events.

This election will not change the political control of Tamworth Borough 
Council, but it is a chance to shine a light on what is happening at the 
council. 
So
